goal: Highest-standard Lean formal verification for Hegemon as a PQC privacy chain, with critical theorem targets proved and production-bound at little to no performance cost.
completion_method: Weighted average of property completion percentages. A property reaches 100 only when the top-level Lean theorem is proved, assumptions are explicit, production conformance/refinement gates bind the shipped path, formal-core includes the gate, and performance-sensitive runtime changes are benchmarked or demonstrably cheap.

last_full_formal_core.commit: working tree after wallet plaintext-commitment package

last_coordinator_audit.summary: Audits confirmed strong Lean hygiene, admission/vector framework, nullifier and balance guardrails, parser/canonical encoding evidence, replay/admission/network posture, and release gates. They also confirmed the highest-value gaps remain proof-system soundness, spend authorization, privacy/confidentiality games, bridge production mint safety, cryptographic primitive assumptions, and full Rust/native-node refinement.
critical_open_gaps: No end-to-end theorem that accepted spends imply authorized private witnesses.
No in-repo proof-system soundness theorem for the deployed proving stack or full AIR-to-spec equivalence.
No formal privacy, zero-knowledge, unlinkability, or note-ciphertext confidentiality game.
No complete Rust/native-node refinement from raw network/RPC bytes through storage, reorg, restart, and accepted state publication.
Bridge local replay binding is stronger than production mint safety; external proof soundness and release verifier posture remain limiting.

last_theorem_slice.summary: Added a wallet note-ciphertext decrypt/plaintext binding slice: Lean now models decrypt admission as metadata gates followed by an explicit crypto-authentication predicate, proving metadata mismatches reject before crypto, crypto failure rejects after matching metadata, decrypt success implies version/crypto-suite/diversifier agreement plus crypto authentication, and decrypted wallet plaintext plus recipient material exports the exact note data whose commitment is the spend-authorization note commitment relation. Production Rust regressions cover wallet and synthetic-crypto wrong-recipient/wrong-key and KEM/note/memo malleation failures, full-view decrypted NoteData/input/output witness commitment equality, and recipient-decrypted wallet bundle/native tx-leaf commitment consistency.

validation.lean_formal: targeted NoteCiphertextDecrypt and lake build Hegemon passed

known_limitations: The decrypt theorem uses an explicit cryptoAuthenticates predicate; it does not prove ML-KEM encapsulation/decapsulation security, AEAD confidentiality or unforgeability, or ciphertext indistinguishability.

The slice proves abstract plaintext-to-commitment binding through the modeled spend-authorization commitment relation, but it does not prove concrete Poseidon2/BLAKE3 commitment implementation equivalence, nullifier derivation, ciphertext-hash security, wallet key custody, or complete wallet/native-node equivalence.
The proof and regressions are performance-neutral because they add Lean evidence and tests only.
